metric_definition.description: Retention at 10% into the video — measures hook strength. How many people are still watching 1/10 of the way through?
metric_definition.what_to_extract: retentionCurve[10].retention — the retention value at index 10 (= 10% through video)

result.conclusion: No meaningful linear relationship found between hook_retention_pct and views (r=-0.050, 95% CI [-0.151, +0.052], p=0.3379, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.121 also confirms no rank-order relationship. This indicator does not predict view count in Tyler's corpus of 370 videos.
result.practical_insight: Retention at 10% into the video does not meaningfully predict views. Safe to deprioritize.

metric_definition.description: Average retention in the final 5% of the video — end-completion signal. Videos that hold viewers to the very end.

result.conclusion: Average retention in the final 5% of the video — end-completion signal. Videos that hold viewers to the very end. Is moderately positively correlated with views (r=+0.472, 95% CI [+0.388, +0.547], p=0.0000, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.472 confirms a linear relationship. Higher final_5pct_retention values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Maximize average retention in the final 5% of the video to increase views.

metric_definition.description: Largest single-step drop in retention anywhere in the video — the worst moment of viewer loss.
metric_definition.what_to_extract: max(|curve[i] - curve[i-1]|) for all consecutive pairs

result.conclusion: Largest single-step drop in retention anywhere in the video — the worst moment of viewer loss. Is weakly positively correlated with views (r=+0.177, 95% CI [+0.076, +0.274], p=0.0006, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.442 confirms a partially non-linear relationship. Higher mid_video_cliff values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Maximize largest single-step drop in retention anywhere in the video to increase views.

metric_definition.description: Shannon entropy of the retention curve — information richness. High entropy means many peaks/valleys vs monotonic decline.

result.conclusion: Shannon entropy of the retention curve — information richness. High entropy means many peaks/valleys vs monotonic decline. Is moderately positively correlated with views (r=+0.465, 95% CI [+0.381, +0.541], p=0.0000, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.556 confirms a linear relationship. Higher retention_entropy values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Maximize shannon entropy of the retention curve to increase views.

metric_definition.description: Slope of retention in the first 10% of video. More negative = viewers dropping faster right after start.

result.conclusion: Slope of retention in the first 10% of video. More negative = viewers dropping faster right after start. Is moderately negatively correlated with views (r=-0.402, 95% CI [-0.484, -0.313], p=0.0000, n=370). Spearman rho=-0.391 confirms a linear relationship. Lower hook_drop_rate values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Minimize slope of retention in the first 10% of video. more negative = viewers dropping faster right after start. to increase views.

metric_definition.description: Retention at 5% into the video.

result.conclusion: No meaningful linear relationship found between retention_pct_5 and views (r=-0.039, 95% CI [-0.141, +0.063], p=0.4532, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.159 also confirms no rank-order relationship. This indicator does not predict view count in Tyler's corpus of 370 videos.
result.practical_insight: Retention at 5% into the video. does not meaningfully predict views. Safe to deprioritize.

result.practical_insight: Retention at 10% into the video. does not meaningfully predict views. Safe to deprioritize.

result.practical_insight: Minimize std deviation of retention in the 50-100% window (volatility). to increase views.

result.practical_insight: Minimize std deviation of retention in the 25-75% window (volatility). to increase views.

metric_definition.description: Interaction: final_5pct_retention multiplied by retention_entropy.

result.conclusion: Interaction: final_5pct_retention multiplied by retention_entropy. Is moderately positively correlated with views (r=+0.473, 95% CI [+0.390, +0.549], p=0.0000, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.473 confirms a linear relationship. Higher final_5pct_retention_x_retention_entropy values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Maximize interaction: final_5pct_retention multiplied by retention_entropy. to increase views.

label: Hook Drop Rate X Final 5Pct Retention

metric_definition.description: Interaction: hook_drop_rate multiplied by final_5pct_retention.

result.conclusion: Interaction: hook_drop_rate multiplied by final_5pct_retention. Is moderately negatively correlated with views (r=-0.473, 95% CI [-0.549, -0.390], p=0.0000, n=370). Spearman rho=-0.472 confirms a linear relationship. Lower hook_drop_rate_x_final_5pct_retention values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Minimize interaction: hook_drop_rate multiplied by final_5pct_retention. to increase views.

metric_definition.description: Interaction: retention_slope_0_25 multiplied by retention_slope_75_100.

result.conclusion: No meaningful linear relationship found between retention_slope_0_25_x_retention_slope_75_100 and views (r=-0.086, 95% CI [-0.186, +0.016], p=0.0980, n=370). Spearman rho=-0.090 also confirms no rank-order relationship. This indicator does not predict view count in Tyler's corpus of 370 videos.
result.practical_insight: Interaction: retention_slope_0_25 multiplied by retention_slope_75_100. does not meaningfully predict views. Safe to deprioritize.

metric_definition.description: Total word count of the video transcript — proxy for how much was said.

result.conclusion: Total word count of the video transcript — proxy for how much was said. Is weakly positively correlated with views (r=+0.264, 95% CI [+0.166, +0.356], p=0.0000, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.279 confirms a linear relationship. Higher transcript_word_count values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Maximize total word count of the video transcript to increase views.

metric_definition.description: Number of questions asked in the transcript — curiosity-gap and engagement signal.

result.conclusion: No meaningful linear relationship found between question_count and views (r=+0.036, 95% CI [-0.067, +0.137], p=0.4943, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.099 also confirms no rank-order relationship. This indicator does not predict view count in Tyler's corpus of 370 videos.
result.practical_insight: Number of questions asked in the transcript does not meaningfully predict views. Safe to deprioritize.

metric_definition.description: Words spoken per second — pacing signal. Fast = dense info, slow = deliberate.

result.conclusion: No meaningful linear relationship found between speech_rate_wps and views (r=+0.095, 95% CI [-0.007, +0.196], p=0.0667, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.021 also confirms no rank-order relationship. This indicator does not predict view count in Tyler's corpus of 370 videos.
result.practical_insight: Words spoken per second does not meaningfully predict views. Safe to deprioritize.

metric_definition.description: Total character count of the transcript — raw verbosity signal.

result.conclusion: Total character count of the transcript — raw verbosity signal. Is weakly positively correlated with views (r=+0.256, 95% CI [+0.158, +0.349], p=0.0000, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.280 confirms a linear relationship. Higher transcript_char_count values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Maximize total character count of the transcript to increase views.

metric_definition.description: Average word length in the transcript — vocabulary complexity proxy.

result.conclusion: No meaningful linear relationship found between avg_word_length and views (r=-0.085, 95% CI [-0.185, +0.017], p=0.1020, n=370). Spearman rho=-0.022 also confirms no rank-order relationship. This indicator does not predict view count in Tyler's corpus of 370 videos.
result.practical_insight: Average word length in the transcript does not meaningfully predict views. Safe to deprioritize.
